content.title: Real-world identification task
content.body:
You're helping a friend find their belongings at a busy café. They describe what they're looking for, and you need to understand their description.

Common descriptions you might hear:
- 我的包是小的黑色的。(Wǒ de bāo shì xiǎo de hēisè de.) - My bag is small and black.
- 我的手机是白色的。(Wǒ de shǒujī shì báisè de.)

content.title: The colour blue (蓝 lán)
content.body:
蓝 (lán) means "blue" in Chinese.

Pronunciation: lán (second tone)
Character note: 蓝 has the grass radical 艹 on top

Examples:
- 蓝色 (lánsè) - the colour blue
- 天蓝色 (tiānlánsè) - sky blue

content.title: Combining size and colour descriptions
content.body:
When using multiple adjectives, list them before 的, separated by commas or without connectors. The most common pattern is: [size] + [colour] + 的 + [noun].

Examples:
- 大红色的苹果 (dà hóngsè de píngguǒ) - big red apple
- 小白色的狗 (xiǎo báisè de gǒu) - small white dog
- 长黑色的头发 (cháng hēisè de tóufa) - long b

content.title: Fat and thin (胖 and 瘦)
content.body:
胖 (pàng) means "fat" or "plump," while 瘦 (shòu) means "thin" or "skinny."

胖 - pàng (fourth tone)
瘦 - shòu (fourth tone)

These adjectives describe body size or weight.

Examples:
- 胖猫 (pàng māo) - fat cat
- 瘦的人 (shòu de rén) - thin person

content.title: The colour pink (粉 fěn)
content.body:
粉 (fěn) primarily means "powder" in Chinese. In colour contexts, it typically refers to pink when used as 粉色 (fěnsè) or 粉红色 (fěnhóngsè), literally "powder-red colour." Alone, 粉 refers to powder rather than the colour.

Pronunciation: fěn (third tone)
Character note: 粉 contains the rice radical 米

Ex

content.title: Describing objects with colours
content.body:
To describe an object's colour in Chinese, use the pattern: [colour] + 的 + [noun] or [colour + 色] + 的 + [noun].

Pattern 1: 红的衣服 (hóng de yīfu) - red clothes
Pattern 2: 红色的衣服 (hóngsè de yīfu) - red clothes

Both patterns are correct. The second is more formal.

More examples:
- 白色的猫 (báisè de māo) -

content.title: The colour red (红 hóng)
content.body:
红 (hóng) means "red" in Chinese. It's one of the most culturally significant colours in Chinese culture, often associated with good fortune and celebration.

Pronunciation: hóng (second tone)
Character breakdown: 红 consists of the silk radical 纟and 工. Note: This is the simplified Chinese form. The t

content.title: The colour white (白 bái)
content.body:
白 (bái) means "white" in Chinese. It can also mean "plain" or "blank."

Pronunciation: bái (second tone)
Character note: 白 is a pictographic character representing a white bone or skull, not a sun

Examples:
- 白色 (báisè) - the colour white
- 白天 (báitiān) - daytime (literally "white day")

content.title: The attributive marker 的 (de)
content.body:
When an adjective modifies a noun in Chinese, you often use the particle 的 (de) between them: adjective + 的 + noun.

的 - de (neutral tone)

Pattern: [adjective] + 的 + [noun]

Examples:
- 红的苹果 (hóng de píngguǒ) - red apple
- 大的狗 (dà de gǒu) - big dog
- 高的人 (gāo de rén) - tall person

Note: For single

content.title: Long and short (长 and 短)
content.body:
长 (cháng) means "long," while 短 (duǎn) means "short" (in length).

长 - cháng (second tone) - meaning "long" (note: 长 has another pronunciation zhǎng meaning "to grow" or "senior")
短 - duǎn (third tone)

These adjectives describe horizontal or linear length.

Examples:
- 长头发 (cháng tóufa) - long hair

content.title: Pretty and handsome (漂亮 and 帅)
content.body:
漂亮 (piàoliang) means "pretty" or "beautiful," while 帅 (shuài) means "handsome."

漂亮 - piàoliang (fourth tone + neutral tone)
帅 - shuài (fourth tone)

漂亮 is typically used for women, things, or general beauty. 帅 is typically used for men.

Examples:
- 漂亮的女孩 (piàoliang de nǚhái) - pretty girl
- 帅的男人 (

content.title: The colour yellow (黄 huáng)
content.body:
黄 (huáng) means "yellow" in Chinese. Yellow was traditionally the emperor's colour in China.

Pronunciation: huáng (second tone)
Character note: 黄 contains the field radical 田

Examples:
- 黄色 (huángsè) - the colour yellow
- 黄河 (Huáng Hé) - Yellow River

content.title: Formal vs. casual colour descriptions
content.body:
Chinese has both formal and casual ways to describe colours. Understanding when to use each is important for natural speech.

Formal/explicit (using 色):
- 红色的车 (hóngsè de chē) - red car
- 蓝色的天 (lánsè de tiān) - blue sky

Casual (without 色):
- 红的车 (hóng de chē) - red car
- 蓝的天 (lán de tiān) - blue sk

content.title: Tall and short (高 and 矮)
content.body:
高 (gāo) means "tall" or "high," while 矮 (ǎi) means "short" (in height).

高 - gāo (first tone)
矮 - ǎi (third tone)

These adjectives describe vertical height of people or objects.

Examples:
- 高的人 (gāo de rén) - tall person
- 矮的桌子 (ǎi de zhuōzi) - short table

content.title: Describing people comprehensively
content.body:
Combine multiple appearance adjectives to give complete descriptions of people. Use natural patterns that flow in Chinese.

Examples:
- 他是又高又帅的男人。(Tā shì yòu gāo yòu shuài de nánrén.) - He is a tall handsome man.
- 她是瘦的漂亮的女孩。(Tā shì shòu de piàoliang de nǚhái.) - She is a thin pretty girl.
- 我的朋友很高很

content.title: Distinguishing height vs. length adjectives
content.body:
Chinese uses different adjectives for vertical height (高/矮) vs. horizontal/linear length (长/短). Understanding which to use depends on what you're describing.

高 (gāo) / 矮 (ǎi) - for vertical height:
- People's height: 高的人 (tall person)
- Buildings: 高的楼 (tall building)
- Mountains: 高的山 (tall mountain

content.title: Shopping scenario application
content.body:
Apply colour and appearance vocabulary in a shopping context. Describe what you want to buy using complete descriptive phrases.

Shopping phrases:
- 我想要红色的。(Wǒ xiǎng yào hóngsè de.) - I want the red one.
- 有没有大的白色的?(Yǒu méiyǒu dà de báisè de?) - Do you have a big white one?
- 这个小的蓝色的多少钱?(Zhège xiǎo

content.title: Colour words with 色 (sè)
content.body:
In formal or explicit contexts, colour words are often followed by 色 (sè), which means "colour." This makes the colour reference more explicit.

色 - sè (fourth tone)

Pattern: [colour] + 色

Examples:
- 红色 (hóngsè) - red colour
- 白色 (báisè) - white colour
- 蓝色 (lánsè) - blue colour

Both 红 and 红色 can

content.title: The colour green (绿 lǜ)
content.body:
绿 (lǜ) means "green" in Chinese.

Pronunciation: lǜ (fourth tone)
Character breakdown: 绿 has the silk radical 纟on the left

Examples:
- 绿色 (lǜsè) - the colour green
- 绿茶 (lǜchá) - green tea

content.title: Comparing and contrasting appearances
content.body:
Use appearance adjectives to compare different people or objects. Express contrasts using learned vocabulary.

Common patterns:
- 他很高,她很矮。(Tā hěn gāo, tā hěn ǎi.) - He is very tall, she is very short.
- 这个是大的,那个是小的。(Zhège shì dà de, nàge shì xiǎo de.) - This one is big, that one is small.
- 我的猫是白色的,

content.title: Big and small (大 and 小)
content.body:
大 (dà) means "big" or "large," while 小 (xiǎo) means "small" or "little."

大 - dà (fourth tone)
小 - xiǎo (third tone)

These are opposite adjectives frequently used to describe size.

Examples:
- 大狗 (dà gǒu) - big dog
- 小猫 (xiǎo māo) - small cat

content.title: Describing people with appearance adjectives
content.body:
Use appearance adjectives with 的 to describe people: [adjective] + 的 + [person word].

Common patterns:
- 高的男人 (gāo de nánrén) - tall man
- 漂亮的女孩 (piàoliang de nǚhái) - pretty girl
- 胖的人 (pàng de rén) - fat person
- 瘦的孩子 (shòu de háizi) - thin child

You can also describe someone by name:
- 高的李明 (gā

content.title: Body size adjectives usage
content.body:
胖 (pàng) and 瘦 (shòu) specifically describe body weight or build. They're different from size adjectives like 大 (big) and 小 (small).

For people and animals:
- 胖的猫 (pàng de māo) - fat cat (body build)
- 大的猫 (dà de māo) - big cat (overall size)
- 瘦的狗 (shòu de gǒu) - thin dog (body build)
- 小的狗 (xiǎo

content.title: The colour black (黑 hēi)
content.body:
黑 (hēi) means "black" or "dark" in Chinese.

Pronunciation: hēi (first tone)
Character note: 黑 is a complex character with the fire radical at the bottom

Examples:
- 黑色 (hēisè) - the colour black
- 黑板 (hēibǎn) - blackboard

content.title: Describing a complete outfit
content.body:
Describe complete clothing items using multiple descriptors. Combine colours, sizes, and other adjectives naturally.

Example descriptions:
- 我有一件大红色的衣服。(Wǒ yǒu yí jiàn dà hóngsè de yīfu.) - I have a big red piece of clothing.
- 她穿小蓝色的裙子。(Tā chuān xiǎo lánsè de qúnzi.) - She wears a small blue skirt
